title: [ESI] Lower ESI Channel ports on modules defined in CIRCT to data/valid/ready
body:
For external modules, ESI lowers to SV interfaces since it assumes a user is going to write a module against that generated SV interface. For internally-defined modules, lowering to regular data/valid/ready wire signaling is preferred so as to not preclude further optimization. SV interfaces also bloat the IR.

One of the use cases is for modules which have ready/valid semantics and a previous pass has attached wrap/unwrap ops onto. This will lower all the ESI wraps/unwraps out and end up not creating any ESI wiring.
